vitalStatistics.growthDescription: slow-growing dwarf succulent branches freely to form a small cluster of stems
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: grows up to 4in (10cm) in height and 6in (15cm) across
anatomicalFeatures: thick, fleshy, gray-green leaves
wavy tips
green upper surface
sometimes mottled with purple spots

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: every two weeks or so in the spring and summer
watering.winterRestFrequency: don’t water mid-fall to early spring in cool climates

positioning.lightRequirements: bright sunlight on a south-facing windowsill (but don’t shut behind curtains on cold nights)

feeding.frequency: two or three times in the spring and summer

flowering.easeOfFlowering: if cared for well, will produce small, reddish, tubular flowers on tall stems in midsummer
flowering.flowerDescription: small, reddish, tubular flowers on tall stems

propagation.generalInstructions: Remove individual leaves with the base intact, place on dry potting soil. When roots appear, insert into potting soil, watering about a week later.

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: relatively tall, branching plant; leaves form at the tips of the branches
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: producing a cluster of branches 3ft (1m) or more in height after about two to five years
anatomicalFeatures: flowerlike rosettes of dark purple or almost black leaves
clusters of striking yellow flowers on mature branches

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: about every two weeks in spring and summer

positioning.lightRequirements: direct sunlight

feeding.frequency: two or three times in spring and summer

flowering.easeOfFlowering: will have best chance of flowering if placed in full sun in cooler climates
flowering.flowerDescription: clusters of striking yellow flowers

propagation.generalInstructions: Take cuttings in spring or summer, lay on fresh, dry potting soil. When new roots appear, plant carefully, supporting each cutting with a stick.

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: grows long leaves in a rosette formation with a central cream-colored stripe
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: smallest of the species; needs a pot no bigger than 16–20in (40–50cm) in diameter
anatomicalFeatures: sharp, pointy leaves
jagged teeth

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: every two weeks or so in spring and summer
watering.winterRestFrequency: don’t water from mid-fall to spring in cool climates

positioning.lightRequirements: full sunshine, windowsill with lots of light

feeding.frequency: two or three times in the growing period (spring and summer)

repotting.bestTime: early spring a week or two before watering again

propagation.generalInstructions: Remove offset rosettes with a sharp knife, leave to dry for a couple of weeks before repotting. Dust with hormone rooting powder if roots have not formed.

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: grows clusters of triangular, striped leaves in a rosette formation
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: one of the smallest aloes; takes about eight years to make a full-sized rosette; max height and spread 10in (25cm)
anatomicalFeatures: leaves culminate in firm, sharp points
little white teeth lining edges of each leaf

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: every two weeks or so in spring and summer
watering.winterRestFrequency: don’t water from mid-fall to spring

positioning.lightRequirements: sunny windowsill or greenhouse kept above 40°F (4°C); indoors don’t shut behind curtains on cold nights

feeding.frequency: two or three times in the growing period (spring and summer)

repotting.handlingInstructions: wrap soft material around stem when moving to protect spines and hands

flowering.easeOfFlowering: keep dry and cool in winter to encourage flowering
flowering.flowerDescription: reddish-pink flowers on a stem growing from middle of rosette after about a dozen leaves

propagation.generalInstructions: Gently remove offset rosettes when repotting; dust with hormone rooting powder if roots have not formed.

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: starts as young globular plant and becomes thick and columnar
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: takes about 10 years to grow 12in (30cm) tall and 6–8in (15–20cm) wide; indoors can reach 3ft (1m) tall
anatomicalFeatures: strong yellowish spines running down ribs
distinct white woolen flecks on surface

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: every two weeks or so in spring and summer
watering.winterRestFrequency: don’t water from mid-fall to spring

positioning.lightRequirements: sunny spot

feeding.frequency: two or three times in the growing period (spring and summer)

repotting.handlingInstructions: wrap a cloth around it when repotting to protect from sharp spines

flowering.easeOfFlowering: full exposure to sunlight helps flower
flowering.flowerDescription: yellow or rarely white flowers grow at crown after about 4in (10cm) height

propagation.generalInstructions: propagated by growing from seed produced by cross-pollinating two plants

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: columnar cactus forms branches as it matures
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: from 6in (15cm) seedling to approx. 18in (45cm) after 4–6 years, continues to grow taller
anatomicalFeatures: green or gray-green stems with 7–12 spined ribs

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: every two weeks or so in spring and summer
watering.winterRestFrequency: don’t water mid-fall to spring in cooler climates

feeding.frequency: two or three times in the growing period (spring and summer)

flowering.easeOfFlowering: keep healthy and growing steadily to encourage flowers

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: grows from tiny seedling into tall, columnar plant over several years

flowering.easeOfFlowering: keep happy to reach flowering height

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: steadily produces new upright stems from base that begin to hang as they grow

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: every two weeks or so in spring and summer

flowering.easeOfFlowering: keep happy and healthy to encourage bright red, tubular flowers
flowering.flowerDescription: bright red, tubular flowers toward tips of hanging stems once about 6in (15cm) long

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: every two weeks or so in spring and summer

flowering.easeOfFlowering: flowers in late summer when a few years old; blooms last nearly a month

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: Vinelike stems grow from the top of the tuber each year, dying back at the end of the growing season.

partially buried woody tuber
fissured appearance

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: every two weeks or so in the spring and summer, when my potting soil is nearly dry
watering.winterRestFrequency: Don’t water mid-fall to spring in cool climates

positioning.lightRequirements: fairly sunny spot, such as on a southeast- or southwest-facing windowsill

feeding.frequency: two or three times during spring and summer

repotting.bestTime: early spring, at the end of my winter rest

flowering.easeOfFlowering: If you care for me well, I will produce tiny yellow flowers among the leaves of my top-growth when I’m a few years old.
flowering.flowerDescription: tiny yellow flowers among the leaves of my top-growth

propagation.generalInstructions: Can only be grown from seed produced by cross-pollinating two plants.

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: Trailing cactus, growing long, thin stems from the top of the plant.
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: The hanging stems can grow to 3ft (1m) or more in length if regularly transplanted.
anatomicalFeatures: thin-stemmed
trailing stems
potting soil comprising two parts peat or coir to one part bark chippings

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: Water me regularly in spring and summer, easing off in early fall
watering.winterRestFrequency: Keep me drier between late fall and the end of winter

positioning.lightRequirements: dappled sunlight, sunny spot with partial shade

repotting.handlingInstructions: Wrap me in a soft cloth to protect me from damage and to prevent injury

flowering.easeOfFlowering: I flower in the summer once stems have reached about 8in (20cm)

propagation.generalInstructions: Cut off one of my stems when several have formed, divide into pieces about 3in (7cm) long, lay on dry potting soil with a thin top layer of sand to encourage root growth.

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: Usually stays solitary (although many other species will go on to form clusters of rosettes).
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: Rosette can reach diameter of 5–10in (13–25cm). Flower stems grow to height of up to 6in (15cm).

covered with a layer of farina (powdery substance)

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: every two weeks or so in spring and summer, when my potting soil is nearly dry

least spiny species in genus

light green stem underneath

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: about every two weeks in spring and summer, only when potting soil is nearly dry

positioning.lightRequirements: lots of sunshine; windowsill or greenhouse

feeding.frequency: two or three times in the growing period (spring and summer)

flowering.easeOfFlowering: flowers reliably with winter rest, not too much water, and plenty of sunshine
flowering.conditionsForFlowering: winter rest period, not too much water, plenty of sunshine
flowering.flowerDescription: ring of pink flowers near top of stem in late spring and summer

propagation.generalInstructions: If forming clusters, take cuttings of surrounding offsets. Can also be raised from seed.

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: Starts globular, can become slightly columnar with age.
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: Eventually grows to about 12in (30cm) in height and 6in (15cm) in width.
anatomicalFeatures: smooth, green body
few spines
spines grow in clumps, curved and needlelike

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: every two weeks or so in spring and summer, when soil is nearly dry
watering.winterRestFrequency: Don’t water mid-fall to spring in cooler climates

positioning.lightRequirements: sunshine; sunny windowsill or greenhouse

feeding.frequency: two or three times in the growing period (spring and summer)

repotting.handlingInstructions: be careful not to knock off spines

flowering.easeOfFlowering: needs to be kept healthy to produce bright red flowers in summer
flowering.flowerDescription: bright red flowers grow from crown

propagation.generalInstructions: Rarely produces offsets; new plants must be grown from seed.

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: Round body grows first, followed by distinctive cephalium.
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: Grows to about 4in (10cm) in height before forming cephalium.
anatomicalFeatures: cephalium is colorful structure made of wool and bristles
cephalium produces flowers and fruit
fruits are tubular with waxy texture

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: in cooler climates, water only in spring and summer months when soil is nearly dry (every two weeks or so)

positioning.lightRequirements: sunshine; sunny windowsill

feeding.frequency: two or three times during spring and summer

flowering.easeOfFlowering: tiny pink flowers grow from woolly cephalium
flowering.conditionsForFlowering: keep growing healthily with correct feeding and watering
flowering.flowerDescription: tiny pink flowers from cephalium

propagation.generalInstructions: Rarely have offsets; often produce fruit. Seeds can be grown from fruit.

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: Forms a branching plant of flattened pads as wide as it is tall over a few years.
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: Slowly branches to reach about 8in (20cm) in height and spread; may be top-heavy indoors.
anatomicalFeatures: oval, mid-green pads covered with soft, white spines
new pads branch from top of older ones
younger pads may dry up in winter

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: every other week in spring and summer when soil is nearly dry
watering.winterRestFrequency: Don’t water from mid-fall to spring in cooler climates

positioning.lightRequirements: sunny spot; temperatures above 41ºF (5ºC); windowsill or greenhouse

feeding.frequency: two or three times in the spring and summer

flowering.easeOfFlowering: flowers quite readily in summer when a few years old

propagation.generalInstructions: Remove some pads by cutting at joint with sharp knife. Allow cut surfaces to dry, then lay flat on fresh potting soil with light sand covering. Water when roots start to emerge.

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: Produces a cluster of stems; fast and prolific grower.
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: Rarely grows more than about 6in (15cm) in height and spread; eventually produces enough offsets to fill a pot 12in (30cm) in diameter.
anatomicalFeatures: rows of blunt “teeth” in upper part of each stem
mottled, star-shaped flowers

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: about every two weeks in spring and summer, only when soil is nearly dry
watering.winterRestFrequency: Don’t water from mid-fall onward in cool climates

positioning.lightRequirements: some sunshine but not too much; southeast- or southwest-facing windowsill

feeding.frequency: two or three times during the growing period

flowering.easeOfFlowering: flowers readily once a few stems have developed
flowering.conditionsForFlowering: once a few stems have developed
flowering.flowerDescription: distinctive mottled, star-shaped flowers

propagation.generalInstructions: Gently remove offsets when repotting. If center of clump has died, break undamaged parts into smaller clumps and transplant.

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: Usually a clustering cactus forming a sturdy clump of columns over time.
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: Grows to about 2ft (60cm) after ten years; can eventually reach 3ft (1m) in height with stem diameter about 4in (10cm).
anatomicalFeatures: 10–15 ribs per stem
offsets grow at base when plant is 4in (10cm) tall
showy red flowers emerge through thick wool-like hairs on side of column near growing point

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: every two weeks or so in spring and summer when soil is nearly dry
watering.winterRestFrequency: Don’t water from mid-fall to spring in cooler climates

positioning.lightRequirements: full sunlight on windowsill or in greenhouse

feeding.frequency: two or three times in the growing period

flowering.easeOfFlowering: full sunlight helps produce flowers
flowering.flowerDescription: deep red, funnel-shaped flowers grow in summer from top of stem

propagation.generalInstructions: Once offsets are about 6in (15cm) tall, remove in early summer with sharp knife and leave to dry. Prop on fresh, dry potting soil with sand layer. Water when roots appear.

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: Branches and makes a clump of stems, each with its own cluster of leaves.
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: Clumps may grow to 8in (20cm) long and sprawl to about 12in (30cm) in diameter over eight to ten years.
anatomicalFeatures: rosettes of fleshy leaves
leaves 1¼–2in (3–5cm) long and 1¼in (3cm) wide
leaves often tightly packed
gaps appear if plant isn’t getting enough light

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: every two weeks in spring and summer, but only when soil is nearly dry
watering.winterRestFrequency: Don’t water mid-fall onward in cool climates

positioning.lightRequirements: sunshine; sunny windowsill or greenhouse

feeding.frequency: just once at the start of the growing period in late spring

repotting.frequency: only repot when reaching sides of pot
repotting.bestTime: start of growing season in late spring

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: It grows in rosettes.
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: Not explicitly stated for size, but implied compact rosette form.
anatomicalFeatures: cylindrical leaves
purple tips
flowers are yellow to pinkish orange

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: Not explicitly stated
watering.winterRestFrequency: Not explicitly stated

positioning.lightRequirements: Not explicitly stated

feeding.frequency: two or three times in spring and summer

repotting.handlingInstructions: Don‘t water me for two weeks afterward.

flowering.easeOfFlowering: Keep me healthy to encourage my flowers.
flowering.flowerDescription: flowers are yellow to pinkish orange

propagation.generalInstructions: Remove some of my leaves, keeping the base of each one intact. Lay them on dry, fresh potting soil with a thin layer of sand. They will send out roots and a tiny rosette will form.

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: compact, rosette-forming species
anatomicalFeatures: longer, tapering leaves
coral-colored flowers

feeding.frequency: two or three times in spring and summer

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: An initially globular cactus, this will cluster slowly after a few years.
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: grows up to 5in (13cm) in height and 4in (10cm) in width
anatomicalFeatures: spirally arranged ribs
dense covering of fine, golden spines
crown covered with very slim, pale yellow spines

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: every two weeks or so in spring and summer, when potting soil is nearly dry
watering.winterRestFrequency: water occasionally from mid-fall to spring

positioning.lightRequirements: bright light, windowsill or lightly shaded greenhouse

feeding.frequency: two or three times during spring and summer

repotting.handlingInstructions: take extra care when repotting due to delicate spines

flowering.easeOfFlowering: From an early age, I produce golden yellow, funnel-shaped blooms at my crown.
flowering.conditionsForFlowering: A cooling period during the winter helps me to flower the following spring.
flowering.flowerDescription: golden yellow, funnel-shaped blooms

propagation.generalInstructions: Take cuttings from the offsets that develop around my stem. Dry them for a week, then place them on fresh potting soil. When roots appear, put them into the soil, and start watering.

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: Starts as a young globular plant, becoming columnar then clustering freely from the base.
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: columnar stem grows to 12in (30cm) tall or more
anatomicalFeatures: about 30 ribs covered with soft, golden spines
large, bright yellow flowers at apex

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: every two weeks or when potting soil is nearly dry in spring and summer only
watering.winterRestFrequency: Not explicitly stated
watering.overwateringWarning: If roots look brown when repotting, cut them back to white tissue (sign of too much water)

positioning.lightRequirements: sunny position such as south-facing windowsill or greenhouse above 41ºF (5ºC)

feeding.frequency: two or three times in spring and summer

flowering.easeOfFlowering: I produce several flowers over a period of a week or two in spring and summer.
flowering.conditionsForFlowering: Expose me to plenty of sunlight.
flowering.flowerDescription: large, bright yellow flowers

propagation.generalInstructions: Cut away one of my clustering stems in summer when it’s two or three years old. Dry it for a week and put it on fresh potting soil with a thin layer of sand. When roots show, insert it into the soil and start watering.

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: Slow-growing columnar cactus solitary to 36in (90cm) before clustering from the base.
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: Within eight years, should grow to about 24in (60cm) in height.
anatomicalFeatures: blue-silver, almost turquoise stems
contrasting yellow spines
five to nineteen ribs

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: every two weeks or so in spring and summer when soil is nearly dry
watering.winterRestFrequency: Don’t water from mid-fall to spring in cooler climates
watering.overwateringWarning: Overwatering can cause root rot and loss of plant

positioning.lightRequirements: sunshine, sunny windowsill or greenhouse with minimum temperature of 43ºF (6ºC)

feeding.frequency: two or three times in growing period (spring and summer)

repotting.handlingInstructions: Wear thick gloves and wrap a cloth around me to prevent damage to spines

flowering.easeOfFlowering: Flowers are rare in cultivation, appear when about 36in (90cm) tall.
flowering.flowerDescription: white flowers on the side of column, emerge at night

propagation.generalInstructions: Remove offsets when about 8in (20cm) tall. Dry for a week or so, then place on fresh, dry potting soil with a layer of sand. Do not water until roots appear.

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: Short, columnar cactus steadily produces offsets to form a cluster of stems.
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: Individual columns grow up to 3in (8cm) tall and about 3/4in (2cm) diameter; flower stems about 2 1/2in (6cm) long.
anatomicalFeatures: dark green, columnar stem
12–17 ribs
short, pale to dark brown spines
many offsets branch from base
white flowers appear on stems, bloom at dusk or in dark

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: every two weeks or so in spring and summer when potting soil is nearly dry
watering.winterRestFrequency: Don't water mid-fall to early spring in cooler climates

positioning.lightRequirements: sunny location on windowsill or greenhouse

feeding.frequency: two or three times with water in spring and summer

repotting.handlingInstructions: Don't water for at least two weeks afterward

flowering.easeOfFlowering: Can produce beautiful, large, white flowers.
flowering.flowerDescription: large, white, nocturnal flowers that bloom for a single night

propagation.generalInstructions: Remove a few offsets in early summer when at least 2in (5cm) long. Place on potting soil with thin layer of sand. Start watering when roots appear, then insert cuttings gently into soil.

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: Globular and grows quite quickly to make a cluster of stems.
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: Spreads outward but never more than about 2in (5cm) tall.
anatomicalFeatures: dark green stems
contrasting white spines
flowers usually red, may be yellow, orange, or white

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: every two weeks or so in spring and summer when potting soil is nearly dry
watering.winterRestFrequency: Ease off in early fall, do not water in late fall and winter in cool climates

positioning.lightRequirements: sunshine, sunny windowsill or greenhouse

feeding.frequency: two or three times during growing period (spring and summer)

repotting.frequency: every year at first, then every two or three years once mature

flowering.easeOfFlowering: Flowers easily in spring and summer.
flowering.flowerDescription: bright red flowers appear from base of stems when about 1 1/4in (3cm) tall

propagation.generalInstructions: Remove offsets when at least 3/4in (2cm) tall. Leave to dry for about a week before placing on fresh potting soil and a layer of sand. Water once roots form.

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: Trailing plant with flattened stems that droop over sides of pot.
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: Several stems lengthen to 12in (30cm) or more over a few years.
anatomicalFeatures: fresh, scallop-edged segments
buds appear at tips of newest segments

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: water in spring and summer, easing off in early fall
watering.winterRestFrequency: don’t water after flowering in winter months in cooler climates

positioning.lightRequirements: dappled sun, southeast- or southwest-facing windowsill

feeding.frequency: two or three times in growing period

repotting.frequency: when roots have filled the pot
repotting.pottingSoilRecommendation: dry mix of two parts acid soil (peat or coir) and one part bark chippings
repotting.handlingInstructions: Don’t water until early spring

flowering.easeOfFlowering: Pink buds followed by flowers appear in fall or early winter on stems that have grown new segments.
flowering.flowerDescription: pink buds and flowers

propagation.generalInstructions: Make cuttings at stem joints and let dry for a few days. Place on potting soil, inserting each cutting gently into soil when roots appear.

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: Produces a succession of leaf-covered stems, which start to hang as they grow.
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: Hanging stems lengthen to 24in (60cm) or more if regularly repotted.
anatomicalFeatures: blue-green leaves tightly packed along trailing stems

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: every two weeks or so in spring and summer when soil is dry
watering.winterRestFrequency: don’t water from early fall to early spring in cool climates

positioning.lightRequirements: partial shade so full sun does not damage leaves

feeding.frequency: two or three times in growing period (spring and summer)

repotting.handlingInstructions: Repot very carefully

flowering.easeOfFlowering: Keep healthy to encourage clusters of pinkish-red flowers.
flowering.flowerDescription: clusters of pinkish-red flowers from ends of hanging stems in early summer

propagation.generalInstructions: Share in spring or summer by cutting off stems about 2 3/4 in (7cm) long. Lay on dry potting soil with thin layer of sand. Water once roots start to appear.

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: Stems multiply and lengthen to cascade over sides of pot.
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: Stems can grow to about 3ft (1m) in length indoors.
anatomicalFeatures: round, green leaves that resemble peas or pearls

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: every couple of weeks in spring and summer
watering.winterRestFrequency: don't water from mid-fall to spring in cooler climates

positioning.lightRequirements: near a moderately sunny window, not too much direct sun

feeding.frequency: two or three times in spring and summer

repotting.handlingInstructions: Gather stems lightly together when repotting

flowering.easeOfFlowering: With right light, water, and food, produces white tubular flowers in summer.
flowering.conditionsForFlowering: Right amount of light, water, and food.
flowering.flowerDescription: white tubular flowers with a pleasant, slightly spicy scent

propagation.generalInstructions: Cut off length of stem about 2in (5cm) long in early summer. Lay on fresh potting soil with thin layer of sand. Water only when roots appear.

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: Forms a solitary stem rounded in shape or short column.
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: Short column about 5in (12cm) tall and 4in (10cm) wide after ten or more years.
anatomicalFeatures: narrow, wavy, brain-like ribs
pale, flat spines up to 1/2in (1cm) long

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: every two weeks or so in spring and summer when potting soil is nearly dry
watering.winterRestFrequency: don’t water from mid-fall to early spring in cool climates

positioning.lightRequirements: sunny spot on windowsill or greenhouse

feeding.frequency: two or three times during growing period

repotting.bestTime: before watering starts in early spring
repotting.handlingInstructions: Handle with extra care due to strong spines that penetrate gloves

flowering.easeOfFlowering: If plenty of sun, white and purple flowers appear in center of new growth when a few years old.
flowering.flowerDescription: white and purple flowers

propagation.generalInstructions: Slow to make clusters; may have to wait until offsets produced to remove.

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: Globular, growing slowly to make a cluster of purple and green stems.
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: Clump about 6–8in (15–20cm) across after eight or ten years.
anatomicalFeatures: stems 3/4–1 1/2in (2–4cm) in height
groups of short, black spines that look like fishbone
deep pink flowers emerge from base

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: every two weeks in spring and summer when soil is nearly dry
watering.winterRestFrequency: don’t water in late fall and winter in cool climates

positioning.lightRequirements: sunny spot on windowsill or greenhouse

feeding.frequency: two or three times in growing period (spring and summer)

repotting.handlingInstructions: Don't water for two weeks

flowering.easeOfFlowering: Need good exposure to sunlight to keep healthy and encourage pink flowers.
flowering.flowerDescription: pink flowers around base in late spring

propagation.generalInstructions: Remove offsets in early summer when at least 3/4in (2cm) tall. Cut at junction with main stem, dry for a week, then place on fresh, dry potting soil with sand. Water once roots appear.

vitalStatistics.growthDescription: Grows slowly to form a cluster of either globular or short, columnar stems.
vitalStatistics.sizeDescription: Grows to maximum height of about 5–6in (12–15cm).
anatomicalFeatures: spines vary from dark brown to whitish yellow
spirally arranged ribs
large pink flowers from growing point at top of stem

watering.growthPeriodFrequency: every two weeks or so when soil is nearly dry during spring and summer in cooler climates
watering.winterRestFrequency: Not explicitly stated

positioning.lightRequirements: sunny spot such as south-facing windowsill or greenhouse with sunny aspect

feeding.frequency: two or three times in spring and summer

repotting.handlingInstructions: Wear thick gloves due to long, sharp spines

flowering.easeOfFlowering: Should flower without special help when a few years old if exposed to enough direct sunlight.
flowering.flowerDescription: large pink flowers

propagation.generalInstructions: Best to keep to yourself or grow from seed; cuttings can cause damage.
